expectations.evidence: Line 10 contains thesis field: "Meridian OS establishes the operating layer for intent-driven AI development by building persistent project memory and structured recipe execution first, then layering artifact generation, product discovery, and frictionless onboarding on top — converting AI assistants from session-scoped suggestion engines into context-aware development partners."
Timeline contains 3 horizons: near (line 24), mid (line 27), long (line 30).
All feature_refs in timeline use F-IDs: ["F1", "F2", "F3"] for near, ["F4"] for mid, ["F5"] for long. All feasibility entries also use F-IDs (F1-F5).

Feasibility section contains entries for all 5 features: F1 (low risk), F2 (medium risk), F3 (medium risk), F4 (low risk), F5 (low risk). Each has risk_level, technical_risks, blockers, sequencing_constraints, and architecture_impact.

All required top-level fields are present: slug, status, product_ref, thesis, narrative, timeline, feasibility, critical_blockers (with 2 entries), open_questions, risk_summary, exclusions, assumptions.

Thesis uses product/strategy language (operating layer, intent-driven AI development, persistent project memory, structured recipe execution, session-scoped suggestion engines, context-aware development partners). No implementation-level jargon like YAML, JSON, API, database, config files, or code references.
